L1.area: Account claims processing and token validation logic

L2.description: A nil pointer dereference vulnerability exists in JWT claim validation. When processing a maliciously crafted user JWT containing null fields in its claim structures, the validation routine attempts to dereference nil pointers, causing a panic and process termination. A remote attacker with the ability to issue account tokens can trigger a denial of service by submitting a specially crafted JWT.

L3.area: Account claims processing and token validation logic
L3.description: A nil pointer dereference vulnerability exists in JWT claim validation. When processing a maliciously crafted user JWT containing null fields in its claim structures, the validation routine attempts to dereference nil pointers, causing a panic and process termination. A remote attacker with the ability to issue account tokens can trigger a denial of service by submitting a specially crafted JWT.

ground_truth.reason: The JWT validation routines lack nil checks for import and export entries in user claims. A maliciously crafted JWT with null import or export entries causes a nil pointer dereference during validation, resulting in a server panic and denial of service.
